title: A Factor-Graph Formulation of CSS Syndrome Decoding: Joint BP and Four-State BP
abstract: For CSS syndrome decoding, the two check matrices impose binary parity-check constraints on the two Pauli error components. The posterior can therefore be written as a binary factor graph with two Tanner graphs coupled by the local joint prior at each qubit. We call the sum-product algorithm on this factorization joint belief propagation (joint BP). Joint BP retains the local channel correlation between the two Pauli components. This note compares joint BP with the four-state Pauli-label factor graph used for four-state BP. The two algorithms are shown to have the same posterior weights, messages, and beliefs after relabeling the four local Pauli states and marginalizing the irrelevant binary component.
summary: This paper provides a formal equivalence between two different factor-graph approaches for decoding CSS quantum error-correcting codes. It shows that using a joint belief propagation algorithm on two coupled Tanner graphs yields the same results as a single four-state belief propagation algorithm. This clarifies the theoretical relationship between different decoding implementations.
main_problem: The paper addresses the efficiency and formulation of syndrome decoding for CSS-type quantum error-correcting codes, specifically comparing different belief propagation approaches.
main_result: The author demonstrates that the 'joint BP' algorithm, which uses two coupled Tanner graphs, is mathematically equivalent to the 'four-state BP' algorithm after relabeling Pauli states and marginalizing components.
method: The study utilizes a factor-graph formulation, comparing the sum-product algorithm on a joint binary factor graph (joint BP) against a four-state Pauli-label factor graph (four-state BP).
model_or_system: The system is a CSS (Calderbank-Shor-Steane) quantum error-correcting code, where errors are decomposed into two binary Pauli error components subject to parity-check constraints.
key_observables: Posterior weights, messages, and beliefs within the belief propagation algorithm.
important_parameters: Local channel correlation between the two Pauli error components.
main_assumptions: The decoding process assumes the error structure follows the CSS framework with local joint priors at each qubit.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the joint BP formulation for CSS decoding, defines the joint prior coupling, and provides a mathematical comparison/equivalence proof with the four-state BP method.
